strategies.strategy: Generated PNG image asset with HTML callouts
strategies.strengths: Good for atmosphere, concept setting, and fear-reducing metaphor where exact labels are not required.
strategies.weaknesses: Cannot safely carry exact instructional text, token positions, or mechanism boundaries.
strategies.recommendation: Keep for atmospheric aids only. Do not use for exact mechanism diagrams.

strategies.strengths: Best for exact relationships, arrows, token order, bars, boundaries, and accessibility-controlled labels.
strategies.weaknesses: Can become cluttered quickly if too many labels are placed inside the SVG.
strategies.recommendation: Preferred for model-mechanism diagrams, with strict label limits and HTML callouts for nuance.

strategies.strengths: Handles wrapping token chips and multi-row mobile layouts better than cramped SVG text.
strategies.weaknesses: Needs careful fixed dimensions so rows do not drift or overflow.
strategies.recommendation: Keep for text-heavy token/card layouts. Consider for future token-stream visuals.

strategies.strengths: Reusable coded primitives for taxonomy, loop, and comparison diagrams.
strategies.weaknesses: Some early labels were smaller than the new readability standard and need continued mobile checks.
strategies.recommendation: Keep, but follow the v0.27.18 minimum label-size rules.

strategies.strategy: Shared visual aid frame with HTML caption, callouts, key takeaway, and accessible description
strategies.strengths: Strong shared grammar; keeps nuanced explanation accessible and readable below the diagram.
strategies.weaknesses: Can feel repetitive if visual titles/captions are not specific.
strategies.recommendation: Preferred wrapper for all Journey visual aids.

strategies.weaknesses: Clutters learner review and exposes implementation details like pattern, variant, callouts, and marker counts.
strategies.recommendation: Deprecated from learner-facing UI. Keep renderer metadata in docs, JSON, and reports only.

strategies.strengths: Lightweight, dependency-free, and easy to tune for mobile.
strategies.weaknesses: Inconsistent use can produce tiny text or unclear endpoint geometry.
strategies.recommendation: Keep and consolidate into shared styles; avoid new one-off tiny labels.

strategies.weaknesses: Would add dependency weight and may reduce design control.
strategies.recommendation: Do not introduce for current Journey visual aids.
